_comment: BASE CASE 3 — HashKey Group. The LIVE-SOURCES-ON-A-REAL-STARTUP case (non-AMINA).
_role_in_demo: Proves the pipeline works on a REAL private crypto startup with live public data, and that drift spans MULTIPLE dimensions (the connect-the-dots thesis). NOT the dramatic LOW->HIGH flip (that's fictional Meridian). HashKey onboards ELEVATED; drift = neutral, legitimate MATERIAL CHANGE -> re-KYC.
_disclaimer: ILLUSTRATIVE of a HashKey-LIKE institutional client. We do NOT imply a real AMINA banking relationship. HashKey is NOT an AMINA customer/partner. All Layer-1 facts are REAL & public (sources in notes); the bank-side risk_score / review cadence / onboarding framing are SIMULATED.
_real_vs_simulated: Identity, ownership, licences, funding and products are REAL & publicly sourced (mark VERIFY on exact dates/figures). risk_score + 'as an AMINA client' framing are SIMULATED.
_structure_note: entity_profile = static identity (not drift-monitored). assertions = the dated beliefs the engine re-validates. Drift signals arrive LIVE from the connectors (funding/news/wayback/gleif).
purpose_of_relationship: Institutional account & treasury for a Hong Kong-licensed digital-asset group (onboarding rationale).
_open_question: State on stage: illustrative of a HashKey-LIKE client; drift framed as 'material change -> re-KYC', never wrongdoing.

risk_model._note: A licensed crypto exchange/asset-manager onboards ELEVATED (MEDIUM), not LOW. Drift = upward pressure from rapid multi-jurisdiction expansion + a new major investor; stays MEDIUM (material change -> re-KYC), it does NOT flip HIGH (Meridian holds the flip).

kyc_review._note: MEDIUM band -> 24-month cadence. The 2024 unicorn round + new investor + offshore entities should pull this OFF-CYCLE — the demo point.
kyc_review._derivation: MEDIUM -> 24mo -> 2023-06-01. The Series A (Jan 2024) + offshore expansion arrived AFTER the scheduled review — exactly the drift a static clock misses.

entity_profile._note: Static KYC identity block. REAL facts (VERIFY exact figures); private group (no listed ticker -> tests the startup path, not SEC).
entity_profile.regulated_financial_entity: Yes — HK SFC-licensed (HashKey Exchange, VATP); later SG/JP/Bermuda/Ireland/Dubai (VERIFY current scope)

assertions.value: Hong Kong-focused licensed digital-asset exchange & asset-management group
assertions.notes: REAL. DRIFTS via expansion into a global multi-product Web3 group (Global exchange, OTC, Cloud, Tokenisation).
BREACHED — now Singapore, Japan, Bermuda, Ireland, Dubai (VARA) + pursuing EU MiCA. Multi-jurisdiction structural drift.
BREAKS — Jan 2024 Series A brought a NEW major investor (OKX Ventures) at a US$1.2B valuation. New significant shareholder -> re-screen UBO/PEP/sanctions.

assertions.value: Spot virtual-asset exchange (HK) + digital-asset asset management
assertions.notes: BREAKS — adds HashKey Global (flagship exchange), OTC desk, Cloud (Web3 infra), Tokenisation, and FUTURES/launchpool via Bermuda. Product-scope drift.

assertions.value: Hong Kong SFC-licensed (VATP via HashKey Exchange)
assertions.notes: BROADENS — adds MAS (SG), JFSA (JP), Bermuda BMA, Central Bank of Ireland VASP (Jan 2025), Dubai VARA (HashKey MENA FZE, May 2025); pursuing EU MiCA. Regulatory perimeter is itself a moving target.
BREAKS — new offshore operating entities (HashKey Global in Bermuda; HashKey MENA FZE in Dubai). Corporate-structure drift -> re-check beneficial ownership chain.
REAL — relevant for an AMINA (crypto-bank) client.
Re-screen on the new investor (OKX Ventures) per HK3.
Stays clean on direct designation; multi-jurisdiction/offshore expansion raises indirect-nexus questions to re-confirm.
Coverage is growth/funding/licensing (neutral). Keep framing neutral — this case is 'material change -> re-KYC', not adverse media.
SIMULATED. 55 = mid-MEDIUM. Demo point = real multi-dimensional material change (ownership + jurisdiction + product + scale) -> re-KYC, NOT a dramatic flip.
